---
title: "ACP Dynamiques de Marché "
author: "abdelhamid,yassine,yahyia"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    df_print: paged
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
    number_sections: true
fontsize: 12pt
geometry: margin=2cm
---


This is an [R Markdown](http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com) Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code. 

Try executing this chunk by clicking the *Run* button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing *Ctrl+Shift+Enter*. 

```{r}
plot(cars)
```

```{r}

library(tidyverse)   
library(readxl)     
library(FactoMineR)  
library(factoextra)  
```
```{r}


library(readxl)

df <- read_excel("data_acp.xlsx")

head(df)
```
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(knitr)

cat("### Colonnes du dataset\n")
colnames(df) %>% as.data.frame() %>% 
  setNames("Nom de la colonne") %>% 
  kable(caption = "Liste des colonnes du dataset")

cat("\n### Aperçu des données\n")
glimpse(df)

cat("\n### Valeurs manquantes par colonne\n")
colSums(is.na(df)) %>% as.data.frame() %>%
  setNames("Nombre de valeurs manquantes") %>%
  rownames_to_column("Colonne") %>%
  kable(caption = "Valeurs manquantes par colonne")

```
```{r}
df <- df %>% drop_na()

df_full <- df

df <- df %>% select(-date, -`Unnamed: 0`)

cat("Colonnes finales :\n")
print(colnames(df))

cat(paste0("\nDimensions : ", nrow(df), " observations x ", ncol(df), " variables\n"))
```
```{r}
cat("Statistiques descriptives :\n")
summary(df)  

cat("\nMatrice de corrélation :\n")
corr_matrix <- cor(df) 
print(corr_matrix)

library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)  

corr_long <- melt(corr_matrix)
colnames(corr_long) <- c("Variable1", "Variable2", "Correlation")

ggplot(corr_long, aes(x = Variable1, y = Variable2, fill = Correlation)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", Correlation)), color = "black", size = 4) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red", midpoint = 0,
                       name = "Corrélation") +
  labs(title = "Matrice de Corrélation entre les Variables",
       x = "",
       y = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

```
```{r}
X <- scale(df)  

cat(sprintf("Données standardisées - Dimensions : %d observations x %d variables\n",
            nrow(X), ncol(X)))

```
```{r}
res.pca_full <- prcomp(X, scale. = FALSE)  # X déjà standardisé

var_explained <- (res.pca_full$sdev^2) / sum(res.pca_full$sdev^2)

cat("Variance expliquée par chaque composante :\n")
print(var_explained)

cat("\nVariance cumulée :\n")
print(cumsum(var_explained))

```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra) 

var_explained <- (res.pca_full$sdev^2) / sum(res.pca_full$sdev^2)
var_cum <- cumsum(var_explained)
composantes <- 1:length(var_explained)

df_plot <- data.frame(
  Composante = composantes,
  Variance = var_explained,
  Variance_cum = var_cum
)

p1 <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = Composante, y = Variance)) +
  geom_line(linetype = "solid", size = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3, shape = 16) +
  labs(title = "Scree plot - Variance par composante",
       x = "Composantes principales",
       y = "Variance expliquée") +
  theme_minimal() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.3)

p2 <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = Composante, y = Variance_cum)) +
  geom_line(color = "red", size = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3, shape = 15, color = "red") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.8, linetype = "dashed", color = "green") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.9, linetype = "dashed", color = "orange") +
  labs(title = "Variance expliquée cumulée",
       x = "Composantes principales",
       y = "Variance cumulée") +
  ylim(0, 1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  geom_text(aes(x = length(Composante), y = 0.8, label = "Seuil 80%"), 
            color = "green", hjust = -0.1, vjust = -0.5, size = 3) +
  geom_text(aes(x = length(Composante), y = 0.9, label = "Seuil 90%"), 
            color = "orange", hjust = -0.1, vjust = -0.5, size = 3)

grid.arrange(p1, p2, ncol = 2)

cat(sprintf("Variance totale expliquée par 2 composantes : %.2f%%\n",sum(var_explained[1:2]) * 100))

```
```{r}
res.pca_2 <- prcomp(X, scale. = FALSE, rank. = 2) 

# Variance expliquée
var_explained <- (res.pca_2$sdev^2) / sum(res.pca_2$sdev^2)

cat(sprintf("PC1 explique : %.2f%%\n", var_explained[1] * 100))
cat(sprintf("PC2 explique : %.2f%%\n", var_explained[2] * 100))
cat(sprintf("Total : %.2f%%\n", sum(var_explained[1:2]) * 100))

```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel) 

loadings <- res.pca_2$rotation[, 1:2]  # colonnes PC1 et PC2
colnames(loadings) <- c("PC1", "PC2")
variables <- rownames(loadings)

var_explained <- (res.pca_2$sdev^2) / sum(res.pca_2$sdev^2)

theta <- seq(0, 2*pi, length.out = 100)
circle <- data.frame(x = cos(theta), y = sin(theta))

df_loadings <- data.frame(
  Variable = variables,
  PC1 = loadings[, "PC1"],
  PC2 = loadings[, "PC2"]
)

ggplot() +
  geom_path(data = circle, aes(x = x, y = y), color = "lightgray", size = 1.2) +  # cercle unité
  geom_segment(data = df_loadings, 
               aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = PC1, yend = PC2),
               arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.2,"cm")),
               color = "red", alpha = 0.7, size = 1.2) +
  geom_text_repel(data = df_loadings,
                  aes(x = PC1*1.15, y = PC2*1.15, label = Variable),
                  size = 4, fontface = "bold", color = "black") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  xlim(-1.3, 1.3) + ylim(-1.3, 1.3) +
  labs(
    x = paste0("PC1 (", round(var_explained[1]*100,1), "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", round(var_explained[2]*100,1), "%)"),
    title = "Cercle des Corrélations (Variables)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14))

```
```{r}
loadings <- res.pca_2$rotation[, 1:2]  
colnames(loadings) <- c("PC1", "PC2")

cat("Loadings (Contributions des variables) :\n")
print(loadings)

cat("\nVariables dominantes pour PC1 :\n")
print(sort(abs(loadings[, "PC1"]), decreasing = TRUE))

cat("\nVariables dominantes pour PC2 :\n")
print(sort(abs(loadings[, "PC2"]), decreasing = TRUE))

```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2) 

loadings_long <- melt(loadings, varnames = c("Variable", "Composante"), value.name = "Loading")

ggplot(loadings_long, aes(x = Composante, y = Variable, fill = Loading)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", Loading)), color = "black", size = 4) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red", midpoint = 0,
                       name = "Loading") +
  labs(title = "Heatmap des Loadings",
       x = "Composantes Principales",
       y = "Variables") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14))

```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)

df_ind <- data.frame(
  PC1 = res.pca_2$x[,1],
  PC2 = res.pca_2$x[,2]
)

var_explained <- (res.pca_2$sdev^2) / sum(res.pca_2$sdev^2)

ggplot(df_ind, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, color = "blue", size = 3, stroke = 0.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  labs(
    x = paste0("PC1 (", round(var_explained[1]*100,1), "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", round(var_explained[2]*100,1), "%)"),
    title = "Projection des Individus"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(alpha = 0.3)
  )

```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)  # pour afficher plusieurs plots ensemble

# Convertir la colonne date en format Date si nécessaire
dates <- as.Date(df_full$date)  # ou as.POSIXct() si tu as date+heure

# Data frame pour PC1
df_pc1 <- data.frame(
  Date = dates,
  PC = res.pca_2$x[,1]
)

df_pc2 <- data.frame(
  Date = dates,
  PC = res.pca_2$x[,2]
)

var_explained <- (res.pca_2$sdev^2) / sum(res.pca_2$sdev^2)

p1 <- ggplot(df_pc1, aes(x = Date, y = PC)) +
  geom_line(color = "blue", size = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "blue", size = 2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    x = "Date",
    y = "PC1",
    title = paste0("Évolution de PC1 dans le temps (", round(var_explained[1]*100,1), "%)")
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(alpha = 0.3)
  )

p2 <- ggplot(df_pc2, aes(x = Date, y = PC)) +
  geom_line(color = "green", size = 1) +
  geom_point(color = "green", size = 2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(
    x = "Date",
    y = "PC2",
    title = paste0("Évolution de PC2 dans le temps (", round(var_explained[2]*100,1), "%)")
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(alpha = 0.3)
  )

grid.arrange(p1, p2, ncol = 1)
```


```{r}
```


```{r}
```















Add a new chunk by clicking the *Insert Chunk* button on the toolbar or by pressing *Ctrl+Alt+I*.

When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the *Preview* button or press *Ctrl+Shift+K* to preview the HTML file).

The preview shows you a rendered HTML copy of the contents of the editor. Consequently, unlike *Knit*, *Preview* does not run any R code chunks. Instead, the output of the chunk when it was last run in the editor is displayed.
